channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump’s $175 billion illegal tariff revenue is now accruing interest, and refund delays could be costing American taxpayers $700 million a month
item.description: “Consumers will be the biggest losers here,” a Cato Institute economist told Fortune.

item.title: The average American teacher makes $72,000, but one in three are so broke they’re taking on side hustles like Uber driving
item.description: Teaching is seen as an AI-proof career by Gen Z—but with the American Dream becoming more out of reach, educators have turned to second jobs to make e...

item.title: Uber CEO says his ‘really demanding’ work culture includes expecting employees to answer his emails over the weekend: ‘Don’t come here if you want to coast’
item.description: Uber boss Dara Khosrowshahi says the most important skill in life is working hard: “I’m not going to let anyone outwork me.”

item.title: Goldman’s top strategist warns stocks are flashing the same warning signs as before the 2008 financial crisis
item.description: Peter Oppenheimer—who correctly called U.S. underperformance and the global rotation—now sees elevated risk of a correction.

item.title: Viral deepfake ad casts Musk, Bezos, and Altman as corpulent overlords powering AI on human sweat. Its creator says the best jokes tell the truth
item.description: The viral AI ad imagines laid-off workers pedaling bikes to power the technology that replaced them.

item.title: Justice Dept., Under Pressure From Trump, Fails to Build Autopen Case Against Biden
item.description: Prosecutors in the U.S. attorney’s office in Washington were unable to build a case, underscoring the department’s increasing inability to follow thro...

item.title: These American Service Members Died in the Iran Conflict
item.description: They were from Nebraska, Florida, Iowa and Minnesota. One was wrapping up his final deployment and hoped to open a martial arts studio.

item.title: Talarico’s Win in Texas Shows That Nice Guys Can Finish First
item.description: At a moment when many Democrats are campaigning on rage and resistance, James Talarico took a different tack in his campaign in the Texas Senate prima...

item.title: Trump Justice Dept. Seeks to Stall State Bar Discipline of Its Lawyers
item.description: The administration has no control over the disciplinary authorities of state bar associations, but a new proposal would let the attorney general ask t...

item.title: Hegseth will headline a campaign rally next week in the home district of a soldier killed in the Iran war.
item.description: The defense secretary planned to appear at a “Top Gun”-themed event for Representative Zach Nunn, Republican of Iowa, in the district where all four f...

item.title: Britain Arrests a Lawmaker’s Husband on Suspicion of Spying for China
item.description: Joani Reid, a Labour member of parliament, said she had never “seen anything to suspect” her husband had broken the law. The Metropolitan Police said ...

item.title: Live Updates: No Letup in Strikes on Iran, U.S. Says, as Mideast Crisis Widens
item.description: American officials said the bombing campaign against Iran was accelerating. European countries deployed assets to the region to protect their interest...

item.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War
item.description: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was set off by the determination of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel to end diplomatic...

item.title: U.S. Submarine Torpedoed Iranian Warship Off Sri Lanka as Conflict Widens
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it was the first such strike since World War II. The Sri Lankan authorities said they had rescued 32 sailors from ...

item.title: Videos Show Homes and Businesses Across Mideast Caught Up by War
item.description: As the conflict widens, residents across the Middle East are feeling the consequences of the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran.
